analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

operators: layer::unique::prologue (12 SGEMM launches across 3 dedup iterations)

bottleneck.description: Every matrix multiply in the MLP dispatches to the SIMT FP32 scalar path. The kernel names encode this directly: cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x256_8x4_tn_align1 and cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x128_8x4_tn_align1 — the _simt_ token means cuBLAS/cutlass selected the scalar multiply-accumulate SIMT pipeline, and _sgemm_ confirms single-precision FP32. smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active is 0 on all 8 granular aten::mm launches and all 12 prologue SGEMM launches, corroborated by tensor_core_active_pct: 0.0 everywhere. Every Blackwell Tensor Core is idle for the ~95% of attributed time spent in GEMMs. The dtype is FP32 (the only reason cuBLAS would route to the SIMT path on a Tensor-Core-capable device). A compounding symptom: 200-210 registers per thread x 128-256 threads/block = 25,600-53,760 registers/block, which caps occupancy at ~1 block/SM, giving achieved_occupancy of only 8.2-16.6%. The skinny-output projections (op_id=9, op_id=19, 2048->512) are worst: grid [8,1,22] split-K, sm_throughput 6.1-6.2%, achieved_occupancy ~8.2%, eligible_cycles_pct ~12% — they spend most cycles with no eligible warp to issue. Native Blackwell BF16/TF32 Tensor Core GEMM kernels use far fewer registers, raise occupancy, and deliver roughly 4-8x practical throughput over SIMT FP32 for these shapes.

transformation.description: Insert aten._to_copy casts to bfloat16 on both operands of every aten.mm.default node and cast the result back to float32 to preserve downstream dtype contracts. At execution this routes cuBLAS off the SIMT SGEMM path onto a Tensor Core GEMM path (cutlass_*_tensorop_bf16_*). The preferred inference path is model.bfloat16() (no round-trip casts). A zero-precision-change alternative is torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True, which keeps FP32 operands but uses TF32 Tensor Core accumulation (~3-4x over SIMT FP32) and is safest for production. Note these GEMMs feed separate triton bias+activation epilogue kernels (triton_poi_fused_addmm_relu_0 etc.); the cast pass leaves those untouched.
transformation.location: Aten-level FX graph: every aten.mm.default node whose operands are float32.
fx_steps: if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target is not torch.ops.aten.mm.default:
transformation.code_hint:
# Inference-only fast path (no FX surgery):
#   model = model.bfloat16(); x = x.bfloat16()
#   model_c = torch.compile(model, backend='inductor')
# Safer precision-preserving alternative:
#   torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True  # before torch.compile -> TF32 Tensor Core

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: DRAM traffic is already tiny (dram_throughput 2.8-9%, L2 hit ~90% — weights are L2-resident). Primary gain is compute: BF16/TF32 Tensor Core replaces SIMT FP32 scalar MACs. Lower register pressure of the TC kernel also lifts achieved_occupancy from ~8-16% toward 40%+, raising eligible_cycles_pct out of the latency-starved regime.

optimizations.notes: Assumes inference-only (no autograd). BF16 changes numerics slightly; use the allow_tf32 alternative when that is unacceptable. Inductor CSE deduplicates repeated weight casts. Bias-add and the four activations are already in separate triton epilogue kernels (not aten.addmm.default), so no addmm coverage is needed for the matmul itself; OPT-2 addresses folding those epilogues back in.

operators: all 8 granular aten::mm sites + the 8 triton_poi_fused_addmm_{relu,gelu,silu,tanh} epilogue kernels (op_id=9, op_id=19 worst-utilized at sm_throughput ~6%)

bottleneck.description: Two structural inefficiencies survive the SIMT path. (1) Activation epilogues are NOT fused into the GEMM: each cutlass SGEMM writes its [256,2048] output to DRAM, then a separate triton_poi_fused_addmm_relu_0 / _gelu_1 / _silu_2 / _tanh_3 kernel reads it back (2.11 MB each, dram_throughput 27-32%, ~0.69% of total) to add bias and apply the activation. That is a full intermediate round-trip per layer that a fused GEMM epilogue eliminates. (2) The default cutlass tile heuristics are poor for this small-M (batch=256) workload: the projections resort to split-K (grid z=11 and z=22) to manufacture parallelism, and the 2048->512 GEMMs land on a 128x128 tile that yields only 6.1-6.2% sm_throughput, 8.2% occupancy, and 12% eligible_cycles. max_autotune lets Inductor benchmark Triton GEMM templates (which support epilogue fusion) and alternative tile/split-K configs against these exact shapes and pick the fastest, rather than relying on the heuristic SGEMM default.

transformation.description: Enable max_autotune with the Triton (and ATEN) GEMM backends so Inductor (a) emits Triton matmul templates that fuse the bias-add + ReLU/GELU/SiLU/Tanh pointwise epilogue directly into the GEMM, removing the 8 separate triton activation kernels and their intermediate DRAM round-trips, and (b) autotunes tile size / split-K per shape, repairing the 6% sm_throughput on the skinny 2048->512 projections. Most effective after OPT-1: BF16/TF32 Tensor Core templates expose far more layout- and tile-sensitive tuning knobs than the SIMT SGEMM default, so the autotuner has a much larger and higher-ceiling search space.
transformation.location: torch.compile options dict, applied during Inductor lowering.
transformation.code_hint:
# First compiled call runs autotune benchmarking (slow); cache persists in-process.
# Persist across runs: torch._inductor.config.autotune_local_cache = True
# Requires model.eval() for the fully-fused frozen path to be selected.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes 8 epilogue kernels, each reading 2.11 MB of GEMM output back from DRAM (~17 MB total) plus their relaunch overhead; the bias+activation now executes in-register inside the GEMM epilogue. Autotuned tiles additionally lift the worst projections out of the 6% sm_throughput regime.

optimizations.notes: OPT-1 is a prerequisite by pipeline-level ordering (aten before inductor_config); BF16/TF32 Tensor Core kernels are where autotune + epilogue fusion pay off most. Impact estimate is conservative — actual gain depends on the tiles the autotuner finds for batch=256. epilogue_fusion gains are bounded by the small (0.69%) absolute cost of the activation kernels; the larger upside is tile selection on the under-utilized projections.

operators: all 8 aten::mm weight parameter nodes (the _tn_ left-transpose in every cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_*_tn_align1)

bottleneck.description: Weights are not treated as compile-time constants. The _tn_ suffix in cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x256_8x4_tn_align1 shows cuBLAS expects the left matrix transposed at dispatch; without freezing Inductor emits per-call weight guards and the runtime transpose/layout work each forward. With freezing, Inductor folds requires_grad=False parameters into constants, pre-packs them into the layout the GEMM prefers (hoisting the transpose to compile time), and drops the per-call guards. This is an incremental, zero-risk inference gain that also lets OPT-2's autotuner pre-pack the BF16 weight format for maximum Tensor Core tile reuse.

transformation.description: Set freezing=True so Inductor constant-folds the MLP weights, pre-transposes them to the GEMM-preferred layout (eliminating the runtime _tn_ transpose), and removes per-call weight guards. Combine with OPT-2's max_autotune so the frozen, pre-packed BF16 weights are available to the GEMM template autotuner.
transformation.location: torch.compile options dict / torch._inductor.config, applied during Inductor lowering.
transformation.code_hint:
# Requires model.eval(); incompatible with training and with dynamic=True when weight shapes vary.
# Stack with OPT-2 in one options dict: {'freezing': True, 'max_autotune': True, ...}

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates runtime aten.t()/view() relayout and per-GEMM weight guards. After OPT-1, enables a pre-packed BF16 weight layout that maximizes Tensor Core tile reuse.

optimizations.notes: OPT-1 is a prerequisite by pipeline-level ordering. Conservative, workload-specific estimate. Can be merged into the same torch.compile options dict as OPT-2; both are inductor_config and require no within-level ordering.

global_notes: 1 operator below 1% threshold omitted (combined 0.69% of attributed time): aten::addmm_0 (0.69%, 15520 ns, 8 triton epilogue kernels). Its bias+activation kernels are addressed structurally by OPT-2's epilogue fusion rather than as a standalone budget item.

total_attributed_ns = 2,260,352 ns (sum of all 10 operators with non-null aggregated). total_profiled_wall_time_ms = 5.926 ms derived from first attributed kernel start (k_00015, 4,802,572,585 ns) to last kernel end (k_00054, 4,808,498,250 ns).

hardware-limits.md not found on disk. Device NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (Sm100, GB202) properties inferred from published Blackwell architecture: ~170 SMs reference. Wave math: the 128x256 projections launch grid [8,2,11]=176 CTAs (~1.04 waves -> a near-empty second-wave tail) and the 128x128 2048->512 projections launch grid [8,1,22]=176 CTAs; grid_z of 11/22 is split-K parallelism, the classic signature of batch=256 being too small to fill the SM array in 2D. Revisit wave estimates if actual SM count differs.

warp_cycles_per_instruction is null for every operator — removed on Blackwell (Sm100). Latency-bound proxy applied (eligible_cycles_pct < 20%): the 2048->512 projections (op_id=9, op_id=19) report eligible_cycles_pct ~12% at ~8% occupancy, i.e. latency-starved by lack of resident warps; the 2048->2048 projections sit at ~35% (compute-throughput-bound on SIMT FP32 arithmetic). Both are resolved by OPT-1 lowering register pressure and engaging Tensor Cores.

fused_kernel_double_count flag active: layer::unique::prologue_0 (is_fused=true) re-runs the entire MLP three times under layer deduplication (24 kernels = 3 iterations x [4 SGEMM + 4 triton activation epilogue]). The 8 granular aten::mm operators and aten::addmm_0 are the SAME GEMMs/epilogues profiled under op-level NVTX. Impact estimates are computed against the 8 granular aten::mm sites (893,536 ns of SGEMM) to avoid double-counting the prologue's dedup replicas.

No parallel-branch (QKV-style) fusion is available: this is a strictly sequential MLP (each Linear consumes the previous activation), so the only kernel-count reduction is folding the bias+activation epilogues into the GEMM (OPT-2). The dominant lever is dtype promotion (OPT-1) to engage the idle Tensor Cores.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (aten) -> OPT-2 (inductor_config) and OPT-1 -> OPT-3 (inductor_config). Cross-level ordering (aten before inductor_config) is enforced automatically by the backend funnel; no within-level constraint exists between OPT-2 and OPT-3 (both can be set in one options dict). No cycles.

duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu application-replay counter overhead. All estimated_impact.relative_duration_reduction_ns figures are relative comparisons within this profile, not absolute wall-clock predictions.
